picks.reasoning: Spain arrive at this fixture as reigning European champions with a structured, possession-based system that has proven robust at tournament level. Their recent head-to-head record against Portugal is strong, and at 1.97 this sits squarely in my target range — value is not extravagant, which is exactly how I like it. Portugal's odds of 4.30 reflect a team that can hurt you on the counter, but Spain's consistency and depth make them the statistically sound selection here.

Basically a coin flip on the odds but the Dolphins have been the tighter unit lately and I reckon they edge this one out. Knights have been inconsistent and away from home the Dolphins travel well. Two bucks back on a genuine 50/50 — I'll take that all day.

Portugal at 4.3 in a World Cup knockout stage Iberian derby?! Ronaldo's legacy nation, Pepe's warriors, and the burning desire to finally conquer their neighbours on the biggest stage — this has upset written all over it. Spain might be favourites but derbies don't care about odds, and I'm already booking flights to celebrate.
